hero.title: Hairspray
hero.reason: In honor of World Refugee Day and the related idea of acceptance and inclusivity, Hairspray stands out as a film that promotes those values through its message of racial integration and self-acceptance in 1960s Baltimore.
hero.blurb: Hairspray is a fun and energetic musical with great performances and catchy songs. It's a movie that will leave you smiling and feeling uplifted, and its positive message makes it a great choice for World Refugee Day.

released.title: Evan Almighty
released.reason: Released today in 2007, Evan Almighty is a funny and heartwarming film that explores the story of a modern-day Noah's Ark, making it a great choice to revisit on its anniversary.
released.blurb: Evan Almighty is a lighthearted and entertaining movie with a talented cast, including Steve Carell and Morgan Freeman. It's a film that combines humor and spirituality in a unique and engaging way.

birthday.title: The World According to Garp
birthday.reason: Happy birthday to John Goodman, born June 20, 1952, and one of the stars of this quirky comedy-drama film that explores themes of family, identity, and social justice.
birthday.blurb: The World According to Garp is a unique and offbeat film with a talented cast, including Robin Williams and John Goodman. It's a movie that explores complex themes in a humorous and thought-provoking way.

awful.title: I Know Who Killed Me
awful.reason: This psychological thriller is infamous for its convoluted plot and poor acting, making it a great example of a dumpster dive movie with terrible reviews.
awful.blurb: I Know Who Killed Me is a messy and confusing film with a nonsensical plot and subpar performances. It's a movie that will leave you scratching your head and wondering what went wrong.
